title: Microsoft Confirmed to Backup BitLocker Keys in Plain-text, Hands Them Over to Law Enforcement
description: Going against standard practice in the industry, Microsoft backs-up your BitLocker Recovery Keys in plain-text, rather than in an end-to-end encrypted form. Apple, on the other hand, backs-up your FileVault Recovery Key end-to-end encrypted as part of your keychain, rendering it entirely unreadable by them and, by extension, law enforcement.

sources.title: Microsoft Gave FBI Keys To Unlock Encrypted Data, Exposing Major Privacy Flaw

title: Canada Computers Website Compromised, Card-skimming Malware Injected
description: Canada Computers website was compromised by an attack who injected card-skimming malware on payment pages. Initially publicized on Reddit by a customer at least 18 days after initially injected, Canada Computers finally took action the same day and began notifying impacted customers 4 days later.

sources.title: Canada Computers customers say retailer’s website compromised their credit cards
Major Canada Computers Breach: Customers Warned to Cancel Cards
Data breach at Canada Computers & Electronics leaks personal customer information

title: President Trump's acting head of CISA uploads documents to ChatGPT
description: The interim director of the US Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, Madhu Gottumukkala, uploaded government documents to a public version of ChatGPT. These documents were marked “for official use only”, and not authorized for public disclosure. CISA flagged the uploads in August of 2025, and conducted an internal review to assess potential impacts. Officials claim that despite the availability of DHS's DHSChat, Gottumukkala strongarmed CISA into giving him access to ChatGPT.

sources.title: Trump’s acting cyber chief uploaded sensitive files into a public version of ChatGPT

title: Dutch Authorities Seize VPN Server Without Warrant
description: Windscribe, a Canadian VPN provider, had one of its servers seized by Dutch authorities, with the promise to return it once they "fully analyze it." Windscribe has a no-logs policy and claims the server taken was using RAM disks, so no private user data should be found.

sources.title: X post from Windscribe about the incident
Dutch authorities allegedly seize VPN server without a warrant — company claims that law enforcement will return it after analyzing the device fully

furtherReading.title: My Big Fat Greek Case (A court case in Greece proving the no-logs policy)

title: Discord Adds Age Verification
description: Discord has announced that they plan to add global age verification to the platform. Discord will attempt to verify users' ages using AI, but will prompt some users to upload a video selfie or government ID. Those who do not verify their age will be restricted to a 'Teen Default Experience' losing access to some features. As of 2026/02/24, Discord has postponed the rollout following user backlash, announced they would no longer be working with Peter Thiel-backed age-verification vendor Persona.

sources.title: Discord Launches Teen Default Experience Globally
Discord Ditches Age-Verification Partner Following User Backlash

title: Critical Notepad vulnerability linked to new Markdown feature
description: The Windows 11 Notepad app, recently upgraded with AI features, now carries a high-severity flaw that exposes users to dangerous attacks. Hackers can trick Windows users into clicking a malicious link inside a Markdown file, opened by default by the Notepad app on most Windows systems. This would then cause Notepad to launch unverified protocols that load and execute remote files/code with the same permissions as the user. The vulnerability, assigned CVE-2026-20841, has a severity rating of 8.8 out of 10. While not directly caused by AI, this vulnerability highlights the risks of rapid feature expansion in established software.

sources.title: Critical Notepad vulnerability reignites criticism of Microsoft’s forced AI features

title: Instagram Ending Support for End-To-End Encrypted Direct Messages
description: Meta has updated Instagram's help pages, stating that E2EE DMs will no longer be supported as of May 8th, 2026. E2EE ensures that messages cannot be meaningfully intercepted by third parties, including Meta, serving as a key mechanism of user privacy. Meta also maintains Messenger and WhatsApp, potentially foreshadowing incoming changes to these platforms as well.

sources.title: Instagram quietly drops end-to-end encrypted chats
